meta.tagline: PSF-aware isophotal PA fitting and GALFIT prep for J-PLUS multi-band galaxy cutouts

runtime.package_manager: pip + conda (cluster ships heavy deps as conda binaries)

components.name: Catalog ingest
components.notes: Synthesizes id from TILE_ID-NUMBER when missing; strips '#' SQL preamble from raw ADQL exports. Required cols: id, A_WORLD, B_WORLD, pa_jplus. R_EFF (pixels) optional but needed for psf-mode auto/on.

components.name: Cutout & PSF discovery
components.notes: Globs images/<id>/fits_images_*/<band>_cutout.fits and images/<id>/psfs_*/psf_*_<band>.fits. Returns Path or None.

components.name: PSF preprocessing
components.notes: Moment-based FWHM. Wiener K = max(1e-6, (bg_RMS / dynamic_range)^2). Loader walks HDU list for first 2D image (handles PSFEx ext-1 layout). Modes: auto | on | off.

components.name: Isophote row selection
components.notes: V6 rule: SMA floor max(3 px, 0.05*half_width); drop pa_err<=0 and ndata<5; min pa_err/sma score. Weak fallback to outermost surviving row.

components.name: Isophote fit + fallback ladder
components.notes: Generates ~57 (eps, pa) configs (catalog prior, orthogonal, perpendicular, 50 MC seeds at np.random.seed(42)) × 2 smoothing levels (sigma=0, sigma=2). Returns best by score=pa_err/sma after keep_best_of=8 strong fits.

components.name: Diagnostic plotting
components.notes: ZScale + Asinh stretch. Per-band PNG with fitted ellipse + caption; 3x4 mosaic per object (red=ok, orange=weak, gray=missing).

components.name: Pipeline orchestrator
components.notes: ProcessPoolExecutor over per-(object, band) tasks. Incremental per-object mosaic finalization once last band returns. BLAS-thread pinning via _worker_init.

components.name: Catalog reconciliation
components.notes: Walks fitted_pa_images/<id>/PA_fits.csv, joins on id. transform_pa_jplus(d) = (90 - d) mod 180 brings sky-frame PA into photutils' image frame.

components.name: Command-line interface
components.notes: Pins BLAS=1 BEFORE importing numpy (must happen in parent process too, not just workers). Five subcommands; two working.

components.name: Package surface
components.notes: 12-band canonical order: uJAVA, J0378, J0395, J0410, J0430, gSDSS, J0515, rSDSS, J0660, iSDSS, J0861, zSDSS.

components.name: Cross-band consensus
components.notes: Weighted circular mean of per-band PAs. Weight = est_ell^2 / pa_err^2 (round galaxies contribute ~0). Eligible bands: status in {ok, weak}. Two-clause outlier rule: a band is flagged only when delta exceeds BOTH k * circular_std (default k=2) AND min_outlier_deg (default 5 deg). The absolute floor prevents tight-consensus galaxies from generating spurious outlier counts.

components.name: GALFIT priors writer
components.notes: Emit GALFIT input files from fitted PAs and ellipticities.

components.name: Source detection (r-band sigma)
components.notes: Sigma-clipped background + N*sigma threshold on the rSDSS cutout. Largest central connected component becomes the detection mask. The mask's second moments seed the EllipseGeometry for ALL bands of that object, replacing the catalog priors as the initial guess.
components.description: rSDSS sigma-clip source detection. Produces DetectionResult (centroid + PA + eps + mask stats) used to seed every band's fit geometry (V0.4) and to build the NaN-clip masks (V0.5).

components.name: Sigma-clipped FITS I/O
components.description: V0.5+. Writes NaN-clipped FITS siblings to images/<id>/clipped_cutouts_<ra>_<dec>/<band>_cutout.fits using the rSDSS-derived detection mask, applied to every band. Provides locate_clipped_or_original() so fit-pa --use-clipped-cutouts can transparently fall back to the raw cutout when no clipped sibling exists.

columns.name: est_pa
columns.desc: Fitted PA in degrees (CCW from +x, image frame)

columns.name: fit_config
columns.desc: Which fallback config produced the winning fit

columns.name: used_weak_fallback
columns.desc: 1 if no isophote cleared the SMA floor

columns.name: is_imputed
columns.desc: 1 if all fits failed and priors were used

columns.name: detect_status
columns.purpose: V0.4: outcome of rSDSS sigma-clip detection for this object.

columns.name: detect_npix
columns.purpose: V0.4: number of pixels above the detect-sigma threshold.

columns.name: detect_sigma
columns.purpose: V0.4: sigma multiple used for the detection threshold.

columns.name: cutout_source
columns.purpose: V0.6: which FITS file the fit actually consumed. 'clipped' = the NaN-masked sibling from make-cutouts; 'original' = raw cutout fallback (no clipped sibling found); 'missing' = neither file existed (the row is imputed/skipped).

columns.name: pa_jplus
columns.desc: Catalog PA, sky frame (east of north)

columns.name: status
columns.purpose: Whether a clipped FITS was written for this band.

columns.name: npix_kept
columns.purpose: Number of finite pixels in the clipped output.

columns.name: detect_x0
columns.purpose: Centroid x of the detection mask, in pixel coords.

columns.name: detect_y0
columns.purpose: Centroid y of the detection mask, in pixel coords.

columns.name: out_path
columns.purpose: Filesystem path of the clipped FITS, or empty on skip.

PA_consensus.csv.rows: one per object (collapse of the 12 bands)

columns.name: pa_consensus
columns.purpose: Weighted circular mean PA across bands (deg).

columns.name: n_bands_used
columns.purpose: Number of bands that converged + passed the outlier rule.

columns.name: n_outliers
columns.purpose: Bands flagged as outliers by the two-clause rule.

columns.name: status
columns.purpose: Composite quality flag based on n_bands_used + resultant_length.

input_layout.<catalog>.csv: ADQL output or pre-processed (id, A_WORLD, B_WORLD, pa_jplus, R_EFF in pixels)

conventions.rule: photutils returns PA in degrees, CCW from +x (image frame). Do NOT apply rad2deg.
All PAs wrapped to [0, 180) before comparison -- PA is direction-only.
Circular diff in [0, 90]: min(|a-b| mod 180, 180 - |a-b| mod 180).
transform_pa_jplus(d) = (90 - d) mod 180 brings sky-frame catalog PA into photutils' image frame.
Per (object, band) tasks via ProcessPoolExecutor. BLAS pinned to 1 thread/worker BEFORE numpy imports.
psf-mode=auto deconvolves when psf_fwhm_pixels / r_eff_pixels >= gate (default 0.2).
If deconvolved fit imputes, retry on raw cutout and label psf_mode='deconv->raw_fallback'.
PSF loader walks HDU list for first 2D image. J-PLUS PSFEx puts data in HDU[1], not HDU[0].
Clipped FITS siblings live at images/<id>/clipped_cutouts_<ra>_<dec>/<band>_cutout.fits, in parallel to images/<id>/fits_images_<ra>_<dec>/<band>_cutout.fits.

`palfitology make-cutouts` defaults to --apply-bands $detect_band (rSDSS only) to keep first-run footprint small. Production array submissions must pass --apply-bands all so fit-pa --use-clipped-cutouts sees clipped siblings on every band.
PA_results.csv carries a `cutout_source` column with values {'clipped','original','missing'}, recording which FITS file each row's fit actually consumed. Use it for QC.
make_summary_mosaic derives one (row_slice, col_slice) from the reference band and applies it to every panel. Per-band re-cropping is the V0.6-era bug; don't reintroduce it.

constraints.rule: Wiener K must be >= regularization_floor (1e-6). Smaller values cause noise amplification on shallow narrowband cutouts.
SMA floor = max(3 px, 0.05 * image_half_width). Below this, photutils returns degenerate sub-pixel isophotes with artificially low pa_err.
R_EFF column must be in PIXELS (not arcsec) when psf-mode is auto or on. Missing column -> warning + fall back to 'raw' everywhere.
12N tasks for N objects. ProcessPoolExecutor with workers = NSLOTS; do NOT exceed cores per node or BLAS contention dominates.
Per-object mosaic is finalized when its last band returns -- mosaics appear throughout the run, not only at the end.
LD_PRELOAD=$CONDA_PREFIX/lib/libgomp.so.1 required on hpc-login (static-TLS dlopen workaround).
`set -u` must come AFTER `conda activate` -- mpivars.deactivate.sh references unbound variables.
qsub -wd or -cwd: SGE inherits the submission directory. Held reconcile job must share cwd with the array tasks.
Cluster ships old GCC (4.8.4). Run pip install -e . inside the 'palfitology' conda env so numpy/scipy/astropy come from prebuilt conda binaries, not source builds.
Sandbox cannot commit/push for the user; always instruct user to commit and push on their laptop.

adrs.title: Refactor V7 monolith into installable package
adrs.context: AutomatedPositionAngleV5/V6/V7.py grew to ~1600 lines. Hard to test, share, or evolve.
adrs.decision: Split into src/palfitology/{catalog,images,selection,fit,plots,pipeline,cli,reconcile}.py with explicit imports. Keep V*.py scripts as read-only reference.

adrs.title: Wiener deconvolution with FWHM gate instead of full convolved modelling
adrs.context: GALFIT downstream does proper convolved-model fitting. Palfitology only needs to give it good PA priors. Full convolved fitting in palfitology would reimplement GALFIT.
adrs.decision: Conservative Wiener deconvolution gated on psf_fwhm/r_eff ratio. When PSF is small relative to galaxy, skip deconv to avoid noise amplification.

adrs.title: ProcessPoolExecutor at (object, band) granularity, not (object)
adrs.context: Catalogs may have only a few hundred objects, but a 40-core node has 40 cores.
adrs.decision: 12N tasks for N objects. Even small catalogs saturate big nodes. Trade-off: 12x more IPC overhead, but bands within an object have no shared compute.

adrs.title: BLAS thread pinning set in cli.py BEFORE numpy import
adrs.context: Workers inherit env, but the parent also imports numpy and would otherwise default to 40 BLAS threads.
adrs.decision: os.environ.setdefault for OMP_NUM_THREADS etc. at the top of cli.py, AND repeated in pipeline._worker_init for each child.

adrs.title: transform_pa_jplus uses pa_corr = 180 - (pa_jplus + (pa_jplus<0)*180)
adrs.context: photutils returns image-frame PA on [0, 180); J-PLUS / SExtractor THETA_IMAGE is on [-90, 90] with the ZERO POINT REVERSED (their 0° is photutils' 180°). The earlier (90 - pa) mod 180 mapping handled the wrap but not the zero-point reversal, leaving ~70% of rSDSS rows mismatched on the v0.2 cluster run.
adrs.decision: Use the TOPCAT-validated two-step mapping: first shift negatives into [0, 180), then reverse direction with 180 - tmp. Empirically collapses the reconciliation scatter onto y = x for the deep broadbands.

adrs.title: psf_mode 'deconv->raw_fallback' rather than dropping the row
adrs.context: On low-S/N cutouts, deconvolution can amplify noise enough that fit_pa_with_fallbacks imputes. Dropping those rows loses information.
adrs.decision: If deconvolved fit imputes, retry on raw cutout. Track outcome in psf_mode column so downstream filtering is possible.

adrs.title: Selection score = pa_err / sma instead of pa_err alone
adrs.context: V4/V5 picked the row with the smallest pa_err. For face-on galaxies this collapsed to sub-pixel isophotes with artificially low pa_err.
adrs.decision: Add SMA floor + use ratio score. Outermost-surviving fallback for face-on cases (weak=True).

adrs.title: Consensus weighting = est_ell^2 / pa_err^2
adrs.context: Inverse-variance alone (1/pa_err^2) lets round galaxies pull the weighted mean even though their PA is intrinsically ill-defined. SMA-based weighting (sma/pa_err) was rejected because SMA is a geometric quantity, not a confidence proxy.
adrs.decision: Damp inverse-variance by ellipticity squared. Round sources contribute negligible weight even when pa_err is small.

adrs.title: Outlier rule is a two-clause AND: delta > k*sigma AND delta > min_outlier_deg
adrs.context: Pure k-sigma flagging on tight-consensus galaxies (circ_std < 2°) produces spurious outliers when one band sits 5° off a 12-band mean. The v0.3 cluster run showed 86% of objects with >= 1 outlier under the pure rule.
adrs.decision: Require an absolute-degree floor (default 5°) in addition to k*sigma. Knob configurable via --min-outlier-deg. After the floor, ~17% of objects had zero outliers; user's interpretation is that this is the floor of what the cross-band variability can support.

adrs.title: v0.4 will introduce r-band sigma detection to seed every band
adrs.context: Many galaxies are detected in rSDSS / iSDSS but invisible in narrowbands; the current fallback ladder spends a lot of work on those before imputing. We also start every band from the same catalog prior, which is loose.
adrs.decision: Add palfitology.detect: sigma-clipped background, N*sigma threshold mask on rSDSS, take the largest central connected component, use its centroid + second moments to seed EllipseGeometry for ALL bands of that object. Conservative version first (seed only, no mask multiplication or cropping).

adrs.title: Detection mask comes from rSDSS only; applied identically to every band
adrs.context: V0.4 needed a single coherent source mask across all 12 bands so the ellipse geometry is consistent. Each band detecting its own source would let faint bands lose the centroid entirely.
adrs.decision: rSDSS is the canonical detect band. detect_source() runs on the rSDSS cutout in the parent process; the resulting DetectionResult is serialised into every (object, band) task dict and used to seed PA, eps, and the bbox crop for the summary mosaic.
adrs.consequences: Objects whose rSDSS cutout is missing or below threshold lose the detection prior for all bands (they fall back to catalog-prior seeding). detect_band is a config knob, not a per-band loop.

adrs.title: Clipped FITS live next to raw cutouts, not in a separate root
adrs.context: V0.5 had to decide where to write NaN-clipped FITS. Options: a separate <root>_clipped/ tree, or sibling folders inside the existing images/<id>/ object directory.
adrs.decision: Sibling folders. Path layout: images/<id>/clipped_cutouts_<ra>_<dec>/<band>_cutout.fits, parallel to images/<id>/fits_images_<ra>_<dec>/<band>_cutout.fits. locate_clipped_or_original() consumes both paths atomically.
adrs.consequences: Simpler --images-root contract (one path argument). Backup/sync of an object backs up both raw and clipped together. Downside: rm -rf of one object dir wipes both.

adrs.title: fit-pa runs all 12 bands independently; cross-band combination is opt-in
adrs.context: Question raised during V0.6 cluster validation: should the pipeline auto-collapse 12 per-band PAs into one consensus PA? Or keep them separate so downstream science chooses which bands to trust?
adrs.decision: Per-band fits stay independent. PA_results.csv carries one row per (id, band). Cross-band combination is a separate subcommand (`palfitology consensus`) that the user opts into.
adrs.consequences: Downstream code filters PA_results.csv at analysis time (`df[df.band.isin(['rSDSS','iSDSS'])]`) rather than relying on a hardcoded trust list. PA_consensus.csv is preserved as an optional output, not a pipeline default.

adrs.title: Summary mosaic uses one shared crop slice across all 12 bands
adrs.context: Pre-fix make_summary_mosaic called _detection_crop per band, which re-thresholded each band's own data against the rSDSS-derived background. Bright and faint bands produced different bounding boxes => different displayed array shapes => inconsistent panel aspect ratios. The fitted ellipse overlay also drifted off the displayed pixels in any band where the per-band crop differed from the reference.
adrs.decision: _detection_crop_slice() is called ONCE against a reference band and the resulting (row_slice, col_slice) is applied identically to every panel. Same idiom make_clipped_summary already used.
adrs.consequences: All 12 panels render at identical shape and the ellipse overlay aligns with the underlying pixels. Side benefit: regenerate-mosaics can re-render historical PA_results.csv runs with the corrected rendering without redoing the fits.

tests.covers: selection.select_isophote (floor, score, weak fallback, face-on collapse)
psf.estimate_psf_fwhm, wiener_deconvolve, preprocess_for_fit (auto/on/off, gate, missing R_EFF, PSFEx ext-1)

scripts.purpose: one-shot driver: chunk -> array -> held reconcile (qsub -hold_jid)
diagnostic for direct vs 180-flipped PA agreement
V0.6 SGE array: parallel make-cutouts across 8 chunks, --apply-bands all, -tc 6 (240 slots).
V0.6 SGE array: parallel fit-pa --use-clipped-cutouts; pair with -hold_jid on the cutouts array.

external_services.name: J-PLUS archive
external_services.purpose: Source of FITS cutouts and PSFs (manual download today; v0.5 automates)

open_questions.question: How to NaN-fill clipped cutouts before fitting without biasing the isophote estimator? photutils.isophote.fit_image fails to converge when an ellipse crosses a NaN boundary, so fit-pa --use-clipped-cutouts still imputes a fraction of rows on real data.
What multi-source heuristic is robust enough for the J-PLUS catalog? The V0.7 plan is radial-profile bimodality; needs cluster validation against a hand-labelled subset before being made the default.
Per-band trust ranking: should the consensus weighting be biased toward rSDSS/iSDSS, or should it stay purely (est_ell^2 / pa_err^2) weighted? Hard to answer without ground truth.

roadmap.feature: Refactor V7 monolith into package + CLI

roadmap.status: shipped; cluster-validated, median R = 0.95 over 20k objects

roadmap.feature: rSDSS sigma-clip detection seeds the fit geometry for every band
`fit-pa --use-clipped-cutouts` consumes clipped FITS with raw fallback

`regenerate-mosaics` subcommand to re-render summary PNGs without re-fitting (refresh after a plots.py change). plots.make_summary_mosaic shared-crop-slice fix so all 12 panels share one window. scripts/run_makecutouts_array.sh passes --apply-bands all.
NaN-fill for clipped cutouts before fitting (photutils.isophote fails to converge across NaN boundaries). Previous attempt was rolled back 2026-05-22; a fresh design is queued.
Multi-source detection. Flag cutouts containing more than one source via radial-profile bimodality so confused fits don't quietly degrade the cross-band consensus.
GALFIT priors writer (galfit.py is currently a stub).
Download integration (AutomatedImageDownloadsV2 port; download.py is a stub).

v0_6_run_results.submission: Array job: 8 tasks of make-cutouts (--apply-bands all) chained via qsub -hold_jid into 8 tasks of fit-pa --use-clipped-cutouts. 240 main.q slots in flight (-pe parallel 40, -tc 6). Submitted from ~/PALFITology_OLD on hpc-login.
v0_6_run_results.open_caveat: V0.6 does NOT fill NaN before fitting. Some fraction of rows will come back with status='imputed' where photutils.isophote hit the NaN boundary; that fraction is what V0.6.1 will measure and fix.
